edges.target: Short Upper Lip with Multiple Frenula
Aplasia or Hypoplasia of the Lungs

nodes.description: Pulmonary hypoplasia or aplasia is a frequent complication of EvC, secondary to thoracic restriction impairing lung development during fetal life.
nodes.id: Aplasia or Hypoplasia of the Lungs

evidence.snippet: HP:0006703 | Aplasia/Hypoplasia of the lungs | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: Atrioventricular canal defect (endocardial cushion defect), including common atrium and complete AV canal, is the most characteristic cardiac malformation in EvC syndrome and occurs in approximately 60% of cases. The defect arises from disrupted Hedgehog-dependent cardiac septation.

evidence.snippet: HP:0006695 | Atrioventricular canal defect | Very frequent (99-80%)

evidence.reference_title: Ellis-van Creveld syndrome and congenital heart defects: presentation of an additional 32 cases.
evidence.snippet: Twenty-eight (88%) had an endocardial cushion defect, with 15 of these having primary failure of atrial septation resulting in CA.

nodes.description: Fusion of the capitate and hamate carpal bones is a characteristic radiographic feature of EvC syndrome, reflecting the abnormal chondral patterning in the hands.

evidence.snippet: HP:0001241 | Capitate-hamate fusion | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: Hedgehog signaling is required for proper atrioventricular septation. Disruption of EVC/EVC2-mediated Hh signaling leads to common atrium and other cardiac septal defects through impaired endocardial cushion development. The cardiac phenotype is strongly reminiscent of heterotaxy syndromes, suggesting a cilia-dependent mechanism.

evidence.reference_title: Ellis-van Creveld syndrome and congenital heart defects: presentation of an additional 32 cases.
evidence.snippet: Twenty-eight (88%) had an endocardial cushion defect, with 15 of these having primary failure of atrial septation resulting in CA.

evidence.reference_title: Ellis-van Creveld syndrome and congenital heart defects: presentation of an additional 32 cases.
evidence.snippet: The frequent association of these abnormalities is strongly reminiscent of the cardiac phenotype found in patients with heterotaxy syndromes.

evidence.reference_title: Ellis-van Creveld syndrome and congenital heart defects: presentation of an additional 32 cases.
evidence.snippet: EVC and EVC2 proteins may be important for cilia function, which is implicated in the pathogenesis of heterotaxy syndromes.

nodes.description: Congenital heart defects occur in approximately 60% of individuals, most commonly common atrium (single atrium) and endocardial cushion defects. Persistent left superior vena cava and pulmonary venous abnormalities are also reported.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in a new gene in Ellis-van Creveld syndrome and Weyers acrodental dysostosis.
evidence.snippet: Congenital cardiac defects, most commonly a defect of primary atrial septation producing a common atrium, occur in 60% of affected individuals.
Heart defects, especially abnormalities of atrial septation, occur in about 60% of cases.

evidence.reference_title: Ellis-van Creveld syndrome and congenital heart defects: presentation of an additional 32 cases.
evidence.snippet: Persistent left superior vena cava (LSVC) and pulmonary venous connection abnormalities were common. The incidence of persistent LSVC and pulmonary venous abnormalities were greater than previously reported for patients with EVC.

nodes.description: Dental anomalies including neonatal teeth, absent or small teeth, abnormal tooth shape, and delayed eruption. Part of the ectodermal dysplasia spectrum.

evidence.snippet: cardinal features are short stature, short ribs, polydactyly, and dysplastic fingernails and teeth.

nodes.description: Short-limb disproportionate short stature. Adult height typically ranges from 109 to 155 cm.

evidence.snippet: Ellis-van Creveld syndrome (EVC) is a chondral and ectodermal dysplasia characterized by short ribs, polydactyly, growth retardation, and ectodermal and heart defects.

nodes.description: Postaxial polydactyly of the feet is a very frequent feature of EvC syndrome, paralleling the hand polydactyly and reflecting the broad disruption of digit number specification by EVC/EVC2 Hedgehog signaling.

evidence.snippet: HP:0001829 | Foot polydactyly | Very frequent (99-80%)

nodes.description: Knock knees (genu valgum) is common and may be progressive.

evidence.snippet: HP:0002857 | Genu valgum | Very frequent (99-80%)

nodes.description: EVC and EVC2 form a complex at the base of the primary cilium that is required for transduction of Hedgehog (Hh) signaling. The complex interacts with Smoothened (Smo) and regulates Gli transcription factor processing. Loss of function of either protein impairs Hh-dependent processes including skeletal patterning, endochondral ossification, and cardiac septation.

evidence.reference_title: The ciliary Evc/Evc2 complex interacts with Smo and controls Hedgehog pathway activity in chondrocytes by regulating Sufu/Gli3 dissociation and Gli3 trafficking in primary cilia.
evidence.snippet: Mutations in EVC or EVC2 disrupt Hh signaling in tooth and bone development.

evidence.reference_title: The ciliary Evc/Evc2 complex interacts with Smo and controls Hedgehog pathway activity in chondrocytes by regulating Sufu/Gli3 dissociation and Gli3 trafficking in primary cilia.
evidence.snippet: Evc and Evc2 are mutually required for localizing to primary cilia and also for maintaining their normal protein levels.

evidence.reference_title: The ciliary Evc/Evc2 complex interacts with Smo and controls Hedgehog pathway activity in chondrocytes by regulating Sufu/Gli3 dissociation and Gli3 trafficking in primary cilia.
evidence.snippet: we found Smo to co-precipitate with Evc/Evc2, indicating that in some cells Hh signaling requires direct interaction of Smo with the Evc/Evc2 complex.

nodes.description: Intrauterine growth retardation is a frequent prenatal feature of EvC syndrome, reflecting the generalized chondrodysplasia affecting fetal growth. It is often detected on prenatal ultrasound alongside the narrow thorax, short long bones, and hexadactyly.

evidence.snippet: HP:0001511 | Intrauterine growth retardation | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: Disproportionate shortening of all four limbs (micromelia) is a very frequent and cardinal feature of EvC syndrome, reflecting the generalized chondrodysplasia caused by EVC/EVC2 Hedgehog signaling dysfunction.

evidence.snippet: HP:0002983 | Micromelia | Very frequent (99-80%)

postaxial polydactyly of the hands, disproportionate short stature with short limbs, dystrophic and/or hypoplastic nails, dental and oral manifestations, congenital heart disease, and radiologic abnormalities (narrow chest, short ribs, short tubular bones

nodes.description: Hypoplastic or dysplastic nails, a component of the ectodermal dysplasia features.

evidence.snippet: cardinal features are short stature, short ribs, polydactyly, and dysplastic fingernails and teeth.

nodes.description: Postaxial (ulnar/fibular) polydactyly of hands, present in nearly all affected individuals. A cardinal feature of the syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in a new gene in Ellis-van Creveld syndrome and Weyers acrodental dysostosis.
evidence.snippet: an autosomal recessive skeletal dysplasia characterized by short limbs, short ribs, postaxial polydactyly and dysplastic nails and teeth.

nodes.description: Kidney abnormalities (including renal hypoplasia and structural defects) are a frequent feature of EvC syndrome, reflecting the broad ciliary dysfunction in EVC/EVC2-related ciliopathy.

evidence.snippet: HP:0000077 | Abnormality of the kidney | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: Short ribs with a narrow thorax that may cause respiratory compromise in the neonatal period.

evidence.snippet: Prognosis is linked to the respiratory difficulties in the first months of life due to thoracic narrowness and possible heart defects.

nodes.description: A short, narrow thorax is a very frequent radiological feature of EvC syndrome, contributing to neonatal respiratory distress and serving as an important prenatal ultrasound marker.

evidence.snippet: HP:0010306 | Short thorax | Very frequent (99-80%)

nodes.description: Short upper lip bound by multiple frenula (mucosal folds) is a characteristic facial feature.
nodes.id: Short Upper Lip with Multiple Frenula

evidence.snippet: HP:0000190 | Abnormal oral frenulum morphology | Frequent (79-30%)
